name: Collado Tornero, Pico Mentiras, Peña Palomera, Los Prados — circular
region: Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima
region_intro: Jogging in Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima offers diverse terrain across limestone plateaus, river valleys, and canyons. The region is characterized by its unique geomorphology, including vast calares with numerous dolines and deep river gorges carved by the Mundo and Tus Rivers. Running routes traverse varied landscapes, from pine and holm oak forests to open areas with elevated slopes and dramatic waterfalls. This natural park provides a challenging environment for trail running, with significant elevation changes and extensive trail networks. Best jogging routes in Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima The most popular jogging route is El Gollizo, Pino Gordo del Toril y Pico del Gallina — circular desde Riópar, a 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Collado Tornero, Pico Mentiras, Peña Palomera, Los Prados — circular, a difficult 14.7 miles (23.6 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing, passing through peaks and high-altitude meadows. Local runners also love the Riópar and Nacimiento del Río Mundo Loop, a 11.3 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through the area around the source of the Mundo River, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Jogging in Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima is defined by limestone plateaus, deep river canyons, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on challenging routes. The routes in Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 450 runners have used komoot to explore Los Calares Del Mundo Y De La Sima's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park?
A: There are over 45 dedicated running routes in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 450 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in this region?
A: The park offers incredibly diverse terrain, from vast, high-altitude limestone plateaus (calares) with intricate networks of sinkholes to deep river valleys and canyons carved by the Mundo and Tus Rivers. You'll encounter paths through pine, holm oak, and gall oak forests, as well as more open areas with significant elevation changes, making it ideal for trail running.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima?
A: While many routes in the park are challenging, there are some easier sections suitable for beginners. For instance, the 1.5 km path leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; is easily accessible and offers a scenic jog. The park also features 13 moderate routes that might be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run than the more difficult options.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the park?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the challenging route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1081126489/el-gollizo-pino-gordo-del-toril-y-pico-del-gallina-circular-desde-riopar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gollizo, Pino Gordo del Toril y Pico del Gallina — circular desde Riópar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 18.5 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083894967/collado-tornero-pico-mentiras-pena-palomera-los-prados-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Tornero, Pico Mentiras, Peña Palomera, Los Prados — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 23.6 km loop with substantial climbing.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I see while jogging?
A: The park is rich in natural beauty. You can jog past the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, where water dramatically emerges from a cave, forming an 80-meter waterfall. Other notable features include the deep canyons of the Tus River, the vast Calar del Mundo plateau, and summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Palomera&lt;/a&gt;, offering magnificent views.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! The most famous waterfall is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, a dramatic 80-meter cascade. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1081123850/riopar-and-nacimiento-del-rio-mundo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar and Nacimiento del Río Mundo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.1 km trail that takes you through the area around this impressive natural wonder. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; within the park.

Q: Is Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima suitable for family jogging or running with children?
A: While many routes are challenging, some sections are suitable for families. The 1.5 km trail to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; is easy and accessible, making it a good option for a scenic family jog. For longer runs, consider the 'Ruta del Agua,' a 10 km route with minimal elevation changes, ideal for a relaxed pace.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima?
A: The park's diverse landscape offers beauty year-round. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for running, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. During periods of thaw, rain, and wind, the 'El Reventón' phenomenon at the Source of the Mundo River is particularly spectacular, with water bursting forth with immense force.

Q: Are there options for parking near the running trailheads?
A: Yes, there are designated parking areas, particularly near popular attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, which has a car park with easy access to the viewpoint trail. It's advisable to check with local interpretation centers for specific parking locations and any potential fees or restrictions for different trailheads.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima?
A: The running routes in Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. Runners often praise the varied and challenging terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the dramatic geological formations like the calares and river canyons.

Q: Are there any less crowded running trails for a more peaceful experience?
A: While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; can be popular, the park's extensive network of trails means you can often find quieter paths, especially on the higher calares or deeper into the river valleys. Exploring routes like the 16 km round trip past Puente la Viga towards Balcón de Pilatos might offer a more secluded experience away from the main attractions.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation near the running routes?
A: The region promotes ecotourism, and you'll find some amenities in nearby towns like Riópar. While specific cafes directly on every trail are unlikely, you can find places to eat and stay in the villages surrounding the natural park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visitor center&lt;/a&gt; can also provide information on local services.
tips:
The Mentiras peak is located in the Calar de la Sima. It is the highest peak in the area and the second in Albacete.

On your way up you find La Sima, which gives its name to this Calar. From its summit it is worth stopping for a few minutes to contemplate the impressive panoramic views, which can be seen across the entire area.

The iconic Peña Palomera is located in the Calar de la Sima, a typical example of a Betic calar. It is a formidable mountain range located between the provinces of Albacete and Jaén. Its relief extends from north to south for more than five kilometers in which it maintains a constant height of around 1800 meters, which gives it a special and superior rank over its neighbors that barely reach 1700 in specific places.

The most characteristic feature of this calar is an enormous molar located in the geographical center of the divide known as the Peña Palomera, while the maximum elevation is the Cerrico or peak of the Mentiras of 1896 meters and which is barely distinguishable in the enormity of the rope, which the locals call Vipers.

El Rincón Cabero is a charming rural farmhouse located in Yeste, in the province of Albacete, Castilla-La Mancha. This old shepherd's farmhouse has been restored and has more than three centuries of history.
Located at the foot of Calar de la Sima and Mentiras, its highest peak, it is ideal for mountain and nature lovers. The house offers a sustainable environment, surrounded by forests, springs and sunlight from which it is supplied.
